title: Printer ink is one of the most expensive liquids you can buy
text:
Drop for drop, printer ink costs more than fine champagne, more than premium vodka, and by some measures more than human blood. A tiny cartridge with a few milliliters inside can run you 30 or 40 dollars.

That works out to over 2,000 dollars per gallon. The printer itself is often sold at a loss, sometimes cheaper than one full set of replacement cartridges. The machine is bait. The ink is the trap.

And many printers now block cheaper third-party cartridges with firmware updates, just so you have to buy theirs. You do not own that printer. You are renting the right to use it.
highlights: renting the right to use it
